commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a macOS memory-pressure monitor that evicts idle OpenAI dashboard WebViews, preserves busy cached WebViews, calls malloc pressure relief, and adds focused cache tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature/performance PR. Current main lacks memory-pressure-specific idle WebView eviction, and the PR source path is clear, but runtime OS delivery is not proven by the PR body.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor-supplied after-fix runtime proof and normal maintainer review; automation cannot produce the contributor’s macOS memory-pressure evidence.

target.title: Release idle WebViews on memory pressure
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a macOS memory-pressure monitor for warning and critical pressure events.
- Evict only idle cached OpenAI dashboard WebViews when pressure arrives, preserving active dashboard refreshes.
- Run malloc pressure relief alongside the WebView cache cleanup.

## Proof

- Real local before profile, installed CodexBar 0.36.1 with trace logging: main app idle averaged 0.152% CPU and 30.6 MB RSS, while the associated WebKit WebContent process held a 306.1 MB physical footprint with a 541.7 MB peak after dashboard work. Samples were idle/mach_msg dominated, pointing to retained WebKit memory rather than a Swift CPU loop.
- After-fix app-path proof added in `426d7b8a`: `Memory pressure monitor evicts idle shared WebViews without interrupting busy WebViews` seeds the shared dashboard WebView cache with one idle entry and one busy entry, runs the `MemoryPressureMonitor` warning handler, and proves the idle WebView is evicted while the busy WebView remains cached.
- `swift test --filter OpenAIDashboardWebViewCacheTests`: 19 tests passed after 2.022 seconds. The focused run emitted the monitor log line `warning com.steipete.codexbar.memory-pressure: level=warning [CodexBarCore] System memory pressure` during the new after-fix test.
- The PR branch app was also packaged and launched from `/Users/alec/Dev/CodexBar/CodexBar.app/Contents/MacOS/CodexBar`. A local `sudo /usr/bin/memory_pressure -S -l warn` / `critical` simulation attempt did not produce CodexBar unified-log entries in this environment, so the PR proof relies on the deterministic app-path test above rather than overclaiming OS delivery evidence.
- `.build/lint-tools/bin/swiftformat Sources Tests --lint`: 0/1140 files require formatting.
- `.build/lint-tools/bin/swiftlint --strict`: 0 violations in 1139 files.
- `swift build --build-tests`: passed.
- `git diff --check`: passed.
- `make check`: locally blocked before Swift lint by the existing `node --check docs/site.js` ESM mismatch under Node v25.9.0; the checked `upstream/main:docs/site.js` starts with an ESM import as well, so this is not introduced by this patch.
target.author: prospectore
